definition: Gas-weighted producers are fundamentally less exposed to stranded asset risk than oil-weighted producers because gas functions as a transition fuel with demand growing through 2035+ on LNG export and AI baseload demand.
evidence: Gas demand growing through 2035+ on LNG export growth
AI data center baseload power demand favoring gas
Gas-weighted producers have structurally different demand trajectory from oil
open_questions: How long does gas transition fuel status persist before renewable baseload displaces it?
Does LNG export demand growth offset domestic gas demand changes?
